rulings.Case Title: CHARLENE DYSART et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND ANSWER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 20, 2008, line 1. DEFENDANT MCNALLY INDUSTRIES, INC'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND ANSWER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is granted. No opposition filed. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion And Special Motion To Strike Deft'S Collection Action As A Meritless Slapp (Ccp 425.16)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 20, 2008, line 2. PLAINTIFF FRANK MARTINEZ'S Motion And Special Motion To Strike Defendant's Collection Action As A Meritless SLAPP (CCP 425.16) is denied. There is no collection action. Defendant is not entitled to an award of fees. The motion to strike is not frivolous. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Void Judgment [Ccp 473(D)]
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 20, 2008, line 2. PLAINTIFF FRANK MARTINEZ'S Motion To Set Aside Void Judgment [CCP 473(D)] is denied. Plaintiff has not shown that the judgment is void. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: NICHOLAS PANOUTSOPOULOS et al VS. PETER KARSANT et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 20, 2008, line 3. DEFENDANT PETER KARSANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the interference with possession and intentional infliction of emotional distress causes of action. Plaintiffs granted leave to file a second amended complaint to allege a fraud claim only. The defense of res judicata does not apply to the fraud claim. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PHILLIP HWEE VS. RONALD P. MAR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion For Attorney Fees Following Appeal
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 20, 2008, line 4. DEFENDANTS RONALD MAR AND JANNIE MAR'S Motion For Attorney Fees Following Appeal. Defendants are awarded $15,000 in fees. The reduction from the amount requested is due to the failure to attach billing records and the failure to explain the basis for the hourly rate. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JANET ANDERSON VS. MERCY MEDICAL CENTER BUILDING REDDING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint To Add Punitive Damages Claim Against Deft Catholic Healthcare West Dba Mercy Medical Center
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 20, 2008, line 5. PLAINTIFF JANET ANDERSON'S Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint To Add Punitive Damages Claim Against Defendant Catholic Healthcare West is granted. The reasons for the prior denial no longer obtain due to the new trial date. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA, A PUBLIC CORPORATION VS. MYRON R. SIEGEL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To S/A Default And Default Judgment; Memorandum Of P/A; Declaration Of M. Roy Siegel; Proposed Responsive Pleading (Motion For Change Of Venue)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 20, 2008, line 6. DEFENDANT M. SIEGEL'S Motion To Set aside Default And Default Judgment is granted. Defendant has shown excusable neglect. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ARTHUR KRALOWEC VS. PAUL, HASTINGS, JANOFSKY & WALKER LLP, A et al*****TRANSFERRED TO ORANGE COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Venue To The Superior Court Of California, County Of Orange [Cccp Sections 395(A), 395.5, 397(A), 399]
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 20, 2008, line 7. DEFENDANTS PAUL, HASTINGS, JANOFSKY & WALKER LLP AND DONALD DAUCHER'S Motion To Transfer Venue To The Superior Court Of California, County Of Orange [CCP Sections 395(A), 395.5, 397(A), 399] is granted. Defendants to pay transfer costs. Defendants' request for fees denied. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS ASSOCIATION INC.,DBA GREAT WESTERN VS. MUKEMMEL T SARIMSAKCI et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 20, 2008, line 8. DEFENDANT MUKEMMEL SARIMSAKCI'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. Plaintiff's request for leave to file an amended complaint is granted, which moots this motion. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 20, 2008, line 10. Counsel for PLAINTIFF SHARON WASH'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. A hearing is required. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: HOMESALES, INC. VS. KAREN HASTIE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 20, 2008, line 16. DEFENDANT KAREN HASTIE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. Notice fatally defective and no opposition was filed. =(302/HEK)
